text_blocks.text: In the aftermath of generally poor
French results in most theaters of the
Seven Years' War in 1758, France's new
foreign minister, the duc de Choiseul,
decided to focus on an invasion of Britain,
to draw British resources away from
militarily and politically, as Pitt again
New France, and sent funds to Britain's
ally on the mainland, Prussia, and the
French Navy failed in the 1759 naval
battles at Lagos and Quiberon Bay. In
one piece of good fortune, some French
supply ships managed to depart France,
eluding the British blockade of the French
